qas.a:
AFI 36-3802 personnel readiness operations
AFPAAS website (AF portal)

The AF web based tool for inintial accounting & ongoing support of airmen after a disaster event

After an event, allows airman to log on & answer....
-"how are you?"
- "where are you?" & "how can we contact you?"
- "What do you need right now?"
Provides tools to command leadership & support personnel for accountability, case management & reporting

-it's a natural or man made disaster
-catastrophic in nature
-potential for wide spread injury or death to airmen & families

Who is included in AFPAAS data?
-active duty
-guard/reservist on Active Duty
-DoD/NAF civilians
-Dependents of AD/Guard/reserve
-dependents of DoD/NAF civilians

Where does AFPAAS data come from?
-periodic refreshes:
From DMCDs DEERS database

DOD event baseline data for affected personnel:
-from DMCDs personnel accountability reporting system (PARS)

Airmen self accounting steps
-when an event occurs
-report status & whereabouts to your command
-log on to AFPAAS
Step 1: self account for event
Step 2: update location & contact information
Step 3: complete needs survey (IF required)

-Commanders designated representative
-Has ability to report status, by name, for all airmen & family members assigned or subordinate to their PAS code in their hierarchy

May also:
-view accounting summaries
-view accounting detail reports
-search for affected individuals
-account for individuals

Has the ability to report status, by name, for all airmen & family members assigned or subordinate to their MPF ID in their hierarchy

May also:
-view accounting summaries
-view accounting detail reports
-search for affected individuals or groups
-account for individuals or groups
(If required, complete needs assessment survey on behalf of affected member)
